title: Singularizing preserving countable additivity quantum channels on quantum measurable cardinals
abstract: We investigate the structural and dynamical properties of a class of quantum channels on von Neumann algebras induced by averaging over operator groups via the Pettis integral.   Utilizing the classical Yosida--Hewitt decomposition, we focus on the interplay between the set-theoretic properties of the underlying space and the topological nature of the resulting quantum states.   We establish a suffucient condition under which a channel preserves $σ$-additivity and exhibits a singularizing property, completely suppressing the normal component of any incoming state.   In conjunction with the theory of Ulam real-valued measurable cardinals, this framework reveals a novel phenomenon: the existence of quantum channels that transform normal states into singular yet strictly $σ$-additive states.   Furthermore, we analyze the structural constraints on the preservation of state purity imposed by the cardinality of the continuum, and extend our constructions to invariant measures on groups and their unitary representations, establishing the convergence of their Cesàro averages in the strong operator topology.
summary: This paper explores the intersection of set theory and operator algebra to describe a class of quantum channels that can transform standard normal quantum states into singular, sigma-additive states. It provides rigorous conditions for when these channels preserve or destroy state purity based on the cardinality of the underlying space. The work demonstrates a novel phenomenon where quantum dynamics can lead to states that are mathematically singular yet still satisfy countable additivity.
main_problem: The paper investigates the structural and dynamical properties of quantum channels on von Neumann algebras, specifically focusing on how these channels transform normal states into singular states.
main_result: The author establishes that quantum channels constructed via Pettis integrals can be 'singularizing' (mapping normal states to singular states) and can preserve sigma-additivity, provided the underlying measure vanishes on singletons and is sigma-additive.
method: The study utilizes the Yosida-Hewitt decomposition, Pettis integration over operator groups, and set-theoretic tools involving Ulam real-valued measurable cardinals.
model_or_system: The mathematical framework involves von Neumann algebras, specifically B(H) and B(l^2(kappa)) for uncountable dimensions, and quantum channels defined by averaging over group representations using the Pettis integral.
key_observables: sigma-additivity, state purity, and the convergence of Cesaro averages in the strong operator topology.
important_parameters: The cardinality of the continuum (c), the dimension of the Hilbert space (kappa), and the properties of the measure (sigma-additivity and whether it vanishes on singletons).
main_assumptions: The construction assumes the existence of Ulam real-valued measurable cardinals and that the dimension kappa is equipped with an Abelian group structure.
paper_structure: The paper is structured by analyzing the construction of channels via Pettis integrals, followed by establishing criteria for sigma-additivity preservation and singularization, investigating the destruction or preservation of state purity, and concluding with the ergodic properties and convergence of channel iterations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
